summary: Cleaned up and matched item collision code in src/melee/it/itcoll.c, mainly by replacing hand-written 4-entry hitbox loops with ARRAY_SIZE-based for loops and helper reuse, correcting it_804D6D1C from a scalar extern to a 4-byte array, using HSD_ASSERT, and naming an embedded Item struct in types.h so it can be referenced locally. The PR reduced duplicate logic around item hitbox collision handling and made several loops clearer while preserving matching constraints with PAD_STACK and an inline helper.

key_files.role: Main item collision cleanup and matching work; simplified collision hitbox loops, added helper reuse, adjusted assert/report code, and cleaned it_80272304.
Named the anonymous Item sub-struct at xB6C as struct xB6C_t so itcoll.c can declare a temporary pointer to entries in xB6C_vars.

what_changed: Changed extern declaration of it_804D6D1C from u8 scalar to u8 it_804D6D1C[4], matching subsequent uses as a per-hitbox counter/status array.
In it_8026F9AC, replaced magic bound 15U with const int log_size = ARRAY_SIZE(it_804A0E70), used log_size in OSReport, and replaced __assert("itcoll.c", 105U, "0") with HSD_ASSERT(105, 0).
In it_8026FA2C, replaced manual index/counter-pointer while loop over four hitboxes with a for loop over ARRAY_SIZE(arg_item0->x5D4_hitboxes), writing directly to it_804D6D1C[i].
In it_8026FAC4, removed duplicated hitbox collision loop and delegated both the ignoreItemID group path and single-item path to it_8026FA2C.
Added static inline helper it_8026FC00_inline(Item* arg_item, HitCapsule* arg_hit, int arg2, Fighter* arg3) to encapsulate the repeated loop that calls lbColl_80008820 on matching enabled hitboxes.
In it_8026FC00, removed duplicated group/single item hitbox scanning and reused it_8026FC00_inline while retaining PAD_STACK(8).
Updated existing pointer initialization sites from &it_804D6D1C to it_804D6D1C now that the symbol is declared as an array.
In it_80272304, replaced a manual while loop and direct user_data access with GET_ITEM(item_gobj), a for loop, and a local struct xB6C_t* tmp before calling lb_8000B1CC.
In src/melee/it/types.h, gave the anonymous struct stored in Item::xB6C_vars the tag name struct xB6C_t.
smart_moves: Using ARRAY_SIZE(it_804A0E70) and ARRAY_SIZE(arg_item->x5D4_hitboxes) removed hard-coded bounds like 15 and 4 while still documenting the exact array being iterated.
Changing it_804D6D1C to an array made the intended per-hitbox indexing explicit and avoided pointer arithmetic from a scalar extern.
Factoring the repeated collision-disable loop into it_8026FA2C and the repeated lbColl_80008820 loop into a static inline helper reduced duplicated control flow without changing caller behavior.
Keeping PAD_STACK annotations while refactoring suggests the cleanup was constrained by decomp matching rather than pure source-style cleanup.
Naming the nested xB6C struct enabled cleaner local pointer use in it_80272304, reducing repeated long field expressions in a matching-friendly way.
Replacing raw __assert arguments with HSD_ASSERT follows project macro conventions and keeps file/line assert behavior centralized.
decomp_lessons: When a global is used as sequential per-index storage, prefer declaring it as an array and initialize pointer variables with the array name rather than &symbol.
ARRAY_SIZE can be safe for matching cleanup when the array object is directly available and the generated bound remains compatible.
For repeated item hitbox loops, a static inline helper can improve readability and reuse while still allowing the compiler to emit matching code.
Named struct tags inside larger structs are useful when decompiled code needs local pointers to array elements of that embedded type.

Do not assume magic constants are semantic enum limits if the actual bound is an array length; this PR replaced 15U with ARRAY_SIZE(it_804A0E70) and removed a speculative comment about HitElement_Lipstick.
Project macros like GET_ITEM and HSD_ASSERT should be used where they express established access/assert conventions and still match.
naming_conventions: The new helper was named after the containing function with an _inline suffix: it_8026FC00_inline.
Loop counters were simplified to i when used as straightforward array indices.
The named embedded struct tag follows its offset-based field name: struct xB6C_t.
The local ARRAY_SIZE bound in it_8026F9AC was named log_size, reflecting the damage log capacity.
Existing project macros GET_ITEM, ARRAY_SIZE, PAD_STACK, and HSD_ASSERT were retained or adopted instead of open-coded alternatives.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved PAD_STACK(4) in it_8026FAC4 and PAD_STACK(8) in it_8026FC00, indicating stack layout remained relevant to matching.
Used static inline for it_8026FC00_inline so shared source logic can still compile into call-site-equivalent loop code.
Changed while loops with manual index increments into for loops where this still matched, reducing source noise.
Introduced a local pointer to struct xB6C_t in it_80272304 to simplify repeated indexed accesses while likely preserving equivalent address calculation.
Replaced duplicated branch bodies with helper calls in the ignoreItemID group path and non-group path, relying on inlining or equivalent codegen for matching.

review_feedback.note: The provided PR slice reports no review comments and no review text excerpt, so no reviewer feedback can be extracted.

follow_up_queries: Find other itcoll.c loops over x5D4_hitboxes that can use ARRAY_SIZE or shared helpers.
Check whether other extern globals declared as scalar u8 are actually fixed-size arrays.
Search for anonymous nested Item structs that should be named to support cleaner matched code.
Audit item collision functions for duplicated ignoreItemID group traversal logic.
Compare HSD_ASSERT usage against remaining raw __assert calls in item code.
